evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: keeping domain outputs consistent, tested, and discoverable
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and maintain the insights layer on top of governed domains, producing reusable KPI models, funnels, cohorts, and segmentations
evidence.jd_authorship: Model key business domains, including merchant activity, product adoption, lifecycle events, and risk scoring, building well-documented, quality-assured data products
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build and maintain the insights layer on top of governed domains, producing reusable KPI models, funnels, cohorts, and segmentations
evidence.autonomy_level: Partner with squads across the tribe on event design and data contracts, maintaining staging pipelines, applying modelling conventions
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Model key business domains...building well-documented, quality-assured data products; Implement technical improvements including incremental processing strategies

velocity_vs_rigour_quote: keeping domain outputs consistent, tested, and discoverable
velocity_vs_rigour_reasoning: Multiple rigour signals emphasizing quality, testing, documentation, and data governance with no velocity language.
domain_risk_reasoning: SumUp is fintech; JD explicitly mentions risk scoring, indicating regulatory and financial reporting implications.
data_team_maturity_quote: Build and maintain the insights layer on top of governed domains, producing reusable KPI models, funnels, cohorts, and segmentations
data_team_maturity_reasoning: Existing governance infrastructure, multiple established business domains, and reusable data products indicate a function beyond early stage that is scaling, not a mature multi-team operation.
jd_authorship_quote: Model key business domains, including merchant activity, product adoption, lifecycle events, and risk scoring, building well-documented, quality-assured data products
jd_authorship_reasoning: Specific technical concepts (data contracts, modelling conventions, KPI models, funnels, cohorts) and concrete business examples show technical depth typical of hiring managers.
stakeholder_orientation_quote: Build and maintain the insights layer on top of governed domains, producing reusable KPI models, funnels, cohorts, and segmentations
stakeholder_orientation_reasoning: The role's primary purpose is building data infrastructure and reusable analytics tools that serve other data consumers and analysts, not directly serving commercial, product, or finance stakeholders.
autonomy_level_quote: Partner with squads across the tribe on event design and data contracts, maintaining staging pipelines, applying modelling conventions
autonomy_level_reasoning: The responsibilities emphasize partnership, maintenance, and application of established patterns, framing the role as executing on defined work rather than independently setting direction.
greenfield_vs_fix_quote: Model key business domains...building well-documented, quality-assured data products; Implement technical improvements including incremental processing strategies
ai_role_reasoning: No AI tools, infrastructure, or GenAI responsibilities mentioned; standard analytics engineering and domain modeling work.
testing_framing_reasoning: Candidate owns building and quality assurance of data products, with explicit ownership verb paired with quality expectation.
loss_aversion_framing_quote: keeping domain outputs consistent, tested, and discoverable
loss_aversion_framing_reasoning: Emphasis on operational consistency and discoverability suggests reliability and pipeline maintenance concerns rather than compliance/audit-primary framing.
